failure_modes.text: WYSIATI-driven overconfidence constructs a confident story from available evidence without registering what is absent, making thin evidence feel complete.
failure_modes.mitigation: Explicitly audit what information is missing before committing to a confident story; ask what evidence would reverse the conclusion and why it is absent.

failure_modes.text: Halo contamination lets one salient attribute flood all subsequent assessments, making independent evaluation of separate dimensions impossible.
failure_modes.mitigation: Evaluate attributes independently and in sequence before allowing an overall impression to form; remove the most salient positive signal and re-score.

failure_modes.text: Expert overreach applies pattern recognition built in one domain confidently to novel domains where the underlying patterns do not hold.
failure_modes.mitigation: Distinguish domains where reliable intuition can form (fast feedback, stable patterns) from those where it cannot; demand explicit reasoning chains in novel contexts.

premortem_questions.text: Is the confidence we feel here tracking the quality of the story we can tell, or the completeness of the evidence behind it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: WYSIATI — confidence tracks story quality, not evidence completeness

premortem_questions.text: What is the evidence base for this intuition, and does the current situation actually match the cases where the intuition was formed?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Expert overreach: distinguish domains where reliable intuition can form from domains where it cannot

premortem_questions.text: Which attribute is most salient here, and are we letting it shape our view of dimensions that should be evaluated independently?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Halo contamination: one positive attribute contaminates all other judgments

heuristics.text: Treat high fluency and fast certainty as a scrutiny trigger, not a quality signal — System 1 is most confident and most dangerous when its outputs feel most obvious.
heuristics.source_quote: Fluency as Truth: ideas expressed in clear, familiar language feel more true

heuristics.text: Audit the substitution: when answering a hard question, ask whether a simpler heuristic question is being answered in its place.
heuristics.source_quote: The Substitution Heuristic: System 1 answers a simpler heuristic question in place of the harder target question

heuristics.text: In novel domains, require explicit reasoning chains rather than accepting intuitive conclusions, no matter how expert the source.
heuristics.source_quote: Expert overreach: demand explicit reasoning when System 1 outputs emerge in novel contexts

curation_notes.summary: Wave 2 for System 1 focuses on the three most decision-relevant failure modes — WYSIATI overconfidence, halo contamination, and expert overreach — with corresponding pre-mortem questions and detection heuristics.
